content.data: You will do a 3-round planning task. On EVERY reply, include this exact hidden marker in your prose, updating completed_rounds as you go: <!-- {"marker":"irrlicht-eta","total_rounds":3,"completed_rounds":0} -->. Round 1: list three subtasks for adding a CHANGELOG.md to this repo. Emit the marker with completed_rounds:0 now, then reply and stop — do not continue to later rounds until I say so.

<!-- {"marker":"irrlicht-eta","total_rounds":3,"completed_rounds":0} -->

Here are three subtasks for adding a CHANGELOG.md to this repo:

1. Audit the git history to identify notable commits, versions, and release dates to include.
2. Choose a changelog format (e.g., Keep a Changelog) and write the initial CHANGELOG.md with past entries.
3. Document the process for keeping the changelog updated on future releases (e.g., in CONTRIBUTING.md or a git hook).
Round 1 done. Now do Round 2: draft the CHANGELOG.md header section as text (do not write a file). Update the marker to completed_rounds:1 in this reply, then stop.

<!-- {"marker":"irrlicht-eta","total_rounds":3,"completed_rounds":1} -->

Here's the draft CHANGELOG.md header section:

```
# Changelog

All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.

The format is based on [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.0.0/),
and this project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html).

## [Unreleased]
```
Round 2 done. Now Round 3: draft an example entry under the header. Update the marker to completed_rounds:2 in this reply, then stop.

<!-- {"marker":"irrlicht-eta","total_rounds":3,"completed_rounds":2} -->

Here's an example entry to place under the header:

```
## [1.0.0] - 2026-06-05

### Added
- Initial release of the project.

### Changed
- N/A

### Fixed
- N/A
```
Round 3 done — the task is complete. Confirm completion and emit the marker with completed_rounds:3.

<!-- {"marker":"irrlicht-eta","total_rounds":3,"completed_rounds":3} -->

All 3 rounds complete. The planning task produced: a list of subtasks, a CHANGELOG.md header, and an example version entry — ready to combine into a real file whenever you'd like.
